Petition For Settlement Of First Account And Approval Of Trustee And Attorney Fees
Off Calendar Jul-18-2023 Off Calendar

Stipulated Petition For Approval Of Settlement Agreement On Consent Of Current Beneficiaries And Remainder Beneficiaries
Off Calendar Jul-20-2023 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Laurelle Gutierrez-Lundo and Katherine Kronick appearing via zoom for HSBC bank USA, N.A. (trustee).
John Golden appearing via zoom for Lee McGrath and Jill Schweizer.

The Court heard no objections for the approval of the settlement agreement. Amended and restated stipulated petition for approval of settlement agreement etc., is taken under submission with the intent to grant. Petition for settlement of first account and approval of trustee and attorney fees is ordered off calendar.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: A. Datt. Deputy: Jaime. Not reported.

Petition For Settlement Of First Account And Approval Of Trustee And Attorney Fees
Off Calendar Nov-03-2021 Continued To Jan-12-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Laurelle Gutierrez, via ZOOM, for HSBC Bank, Trustee; Ryan Cunningham, via ZOOM, for Lee McGrath and Jill Schweizer, Objectors.

The court states its concerns regarding venue in this matter and if it is, why hasn't the Law Firm of Morrison and Forester been notified. Ms. Gutierrez reports that the law firm has not been involved in this matter in several years and that the attorneys who did work on this matter are no longer with the law firm. The court advises Ms. Gutierrez that those attorneys will need to be identified and that the court will be looking for an amended petition and those attorneys will need to be notified pursuant to California probate code 16067.1. The court and Ms. Gutierrez then discuss how accounting issues will be resolved if this is indeed the correct venue. Ms. Gutierrez references probate code 17000 and advises that her client wishes to handle issues with this matter with the primary goal being to resign as trustee. The court and Ms. Gutierrez continue to discuss the several issues with his matter. The court advises Ms. Gutierrez that it appears that she will need to amend her petition if this matter is to move forward and reach a resolution. The court instructs the parties to meet and confer regarding the number of possible trusts that should be involved and if it is determined that there should be multiple trusts, each trust should have its own case number.

The Petition for Settlement of First Account, etc., is continued to Jan-12-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.
